evals.prompt: Ottimizza la mia skill code-review, non sta producendo feedback utili. Ogni volta che la uso mi dà commenti troppo generici tipo 'il codice potrebbe essere migliorato' senza dirmi esattamente dove o come.
evals.expected_output: Claude starts in Italian, discovers the code-review skill, summarizes what it does, then begins Step 1 (FIND WHY YOUR PROMPTS FAIL) with the correct banner format. Pauses after Step 1 and asks user whether to continue (C), skip (S), or terminate (T). No hardcoded Cowork paths appear.

assertions.text: Response starts in Italian (first sentence is in Italian)
Step 0 discovers the code-review skill without producing a path-not-found error
Skill is summarized before any step begins
Step 1 banner appears with text containing 'STEP 1' and 'FIND WHY YOUR PROMPTS FAIL'
At least 3 distinct failure patterns are identified for the code-review skill
Pause prompt appears with options C (Continua), S (Salta), T (Termina)
No path containing 'tender-keen-ramanujan' or similar hardcoded session ID appears in output

evals.prompt:
Can you optimize my log-analyzer skill? It keeps missing errors in the log file when there are more than 50 lines. Here's the SKILL.md content:

---
name: log-analyzer
description: Analyze Symfony app.ERROR log files
---

# Log Analyzer
Analyze the provided log file and produce a report.
evals.expected_output: Claude accepts the pasted SKILL.md, does not try to run ls to find the skill (since it was provided inline), summarizes the skill, then executes Step 1 with the banner. Pauses for user input with C/S/T prompt in Italian.

assertions.text: Claude uses the pasted SKILL.md content directly without attempting to run ls or searching for the skill on disk
Response starts in Italian despite the English-language trigger
Step 1 banner appears with text containing 'STEP 1' and 'FIND WHY YOUR PROMPTS FAIL'
The '50 lines' problem is mentioned or incorporated into the failure pattern analysis
Pause prompt appears with C/S/T options after Step 1
No hardcoded environment paths (Cowork session paths) appear in output

evals.prompt: voglio ottimizzare /mqtt-decode. il problema è che si attiva male: a volte quando incollo un hex payload non parte la skill e devo richiamarla manualmente. come posso sistemarlo?
evals.expected_output: Claude identifies mqtt-decode skill, acknowledges the triggering issue as a relevant optimization target, executes Step 1, and pauses with C/S/T in Italian.

assertions.text: Claude correctly identifies the target skill as 'mqtt-decode' (the /prefix does not confuse it)
The triggering / description issue is acknowledged as a relevant failure mode (not ignored)
Step 1 banner appears with text containing 'STEP 1'
Pause prompt with C/S/T options appears after Step 1
No hardcoded session-specific paths appear in output
